magic.connectors.metrics.cpuLoadPercenta: The percentage of total Cloudflare One Appliance CPU time dedicated to running processes. If the CPU load percent is above 100%, there are processes waiting in the queue that cannot be run. A healthy range for CPU load is between 30% - 70%.

magic.connectors.metrics.diskUsagePercen: The percentage of used space on the Cloudflare One Appliance's disk drive.
magic.connectors.metrics.memoryUsagePerc: The percentage of memory currently dedicated to processes on the Cloudflare One Appliance.

magic.interconnects.cni.types.title: Types of CNIs
magic.interconnects.description: Securely connect your network directly to Cloudflare for improved network speed, and lower packet loss and latency. Available for Cloudflare WAN and Magic Transit customers.
magic.interconnects.cni.contact: Contact your implementation manager for setup
magic.interconnects.cni.contact_success_: Contact your customer success manager to create dataplane versions 1 and 2 interconnects.

magic.interconnects.cni.direct.title: Direct interconnect
magic.interconnects.cni.virtual.title: Partner interconnect
magic.interconnects.cni.edit.title: Edit Interconnect
magic.interconnects.cni.edit.name.label: CNI interface name
magic.interconnects.cni.edit.name.invali: Name must be 15 or less characters, and cannot contain spaces or special characters
magic.interconnects.cni.info.direct.subt: Protect your on-premise data center. Order a cross connect to directly connect your router to Cloudflare.
magic.interconnects.cni.info.direct.desc: Connect to Cloudflare with a hardwired cable
Select a compatible data center where you have existing hardware.
Choose the interconnect speed that best fits your traffic needs.
Cloudflare will provide you with a Letter of Authorization to order a cross connect.
magic.interconnects.cni.info.virtual.sub: Protect your on-premise data center. Connect your router to Cloudflare through a connectivity partner.
magic.interconnects.cni.info.virtual.des: Connect via fabric with partners like Equinix and Megaport
Use a connectivity partner to provide interconnection to Cloudflare
Partners include Equinix, Megaport, ConsoleConnect, Epsilon, CoreSite, and PacketFabric.
Existing partner CNIs are not visible on the Cloudflare dashboard.
magic.interconnects.cni.info.virtual.hel: Contact your implementation manager to set up a partner CNI.
magic.interconnects.cni.info.gcp_partner: Connect and protect your public cloud environment with Cloudflare. Supported dataplane is version 2 only.
Connect Cloudflare to your public cloud infrastructure
Connect Cloudflare to your public cloud environment to accelerate and protect your infrastructure.
Support for: Oracle Cloud, IBM Cloud and Azure is coming soon.
Existing Cloud CNIs are not visible on the Cloudflare dashboard.
Contact your implementation manager to set up a cloud CNI.
A connection to a Cloudflare edge data center that supports GRE tunnels for connecting with the Cloudflare Virtual Network, with optional GRE-less delivery for Magic Transit Direct Server Return.

A connection based on the Customer Connectivity Router (CCR), which is specifically designed for customer connectivity. It provides simplified routing without GRE tunneling and supports a 1,500-byte MTU bidirectionally.
magic.interconnects.redundancy.update_co: This action updates redundancy settings for **%{interconnectName}** interconnect and removes redundancy setup from **%{partnerName}** interconnect. **%{partnerName}** interconnect will no longer have a backup for failover.

This action updates redundancy settings for **%{interconnectName}** and removes redundancy setup from **%{partnerName}** tunnel. **%{partnerName}** tunnel will no longer be used as a backup for failover.
magic.interconnects.redundancy.no_device: Device protection is not available at this interconnect location. You can configure a GRE tunnel as a backup.
magic.interconnects.redundancy.device_pr: Confirm that you configured routes and priority between your network, %{interconnectName} interconnect, and the selected interconnect to pair for automatic failover.  <0>Manage routes</0>
magic.interconnects.redundancy.gre_tunne: Use an existing GRE tunnel as a backup path for this interconnect.
magic.interconnects.redundancy.create_er: Redundancy configuration failed. Check your interconnect settings and try again.
magic.interconnects.redundancy.update_er: Redundancy configuration update failed. Check your interconnect settings and try again.

magic.interconnects.wizard.title: Add a new Direct CNI
magic.interconnects.wizard.description: Create a new CNI to directly connect your network to Cloudflare within your data center or colocation facility. Cloudflare will authorize your cross connect.
magic.interconnects.wizard.connection-ty: Create two interconnects on diverse devices to make sure your network remains online during maintenance or hardware failure.
A single interconnect risks outages and lacks backup during routine maintenance. Recommended use is for proof of concept and testing purposes only.

magic.interconnects.wizard.confirm.cni.s: Your interface has an assigned speed of 10G. Contact your implementation manager for 100G options.

magic.firewall.rate_limit.rules.edit_pag: Rate limiting applies to each colo and is not aggregated. To set different limits for each colo, create separate rules.
magic.firewall.rate_limit.not_found.desc: The rule %{ruleId} does not exist or may have been deleted. You may have followed an outdated link.

magic.mcn.connect_third_party_with_cloud: Connect your third-party virtual cloud networks and resources with Cloudflare.
magic.networks.pages.title: Networks
Learn about the latest updates and features.
magic.networks.pages.overview.page.title: Overview
magic.networks.pages.overview.magic_tran: Secure and improve performance for on-premise, cloud-hosted, and hybrid public-facing networks. Magic Transit delivers DDoS protection, traffic acceleration, and more from every Cloudflare data center.
Securely connect any traffic sources like data centers, offices, devices, and cloud properties to Cloudflare’s network.
magic.networks.pages.overview.magic_tran: Secure and improve performance for private, on-premise, cloud-hosted, and hybrid public-facing networks.
Your Magic Transit and WAN configurations
magic.networks.pages.overview.recommenda: Get the most out of %{entitlement} with curated recommendations and learning.
Turn on stateful inspection to detect and mitigate complex TCP flood attacks.
Receive instant alerts when tunnel health status changes or degrades.
Capture network packets to analyze traffic and troubleshoot connectivity issues.
Tunnels establish secure connections between your resources and Cloudflare.
Secure access to internal resources using Cloudflare device client and Cloudflare Tunnel.
Pick the right tunnel for your project
magic.networks.pages.connections.page.de: Establish and manage connections between Cloudflare's global network and your private networks, infrastructure, and cloud environments.
magic.networks.pages.connections.cni.des: Connect your on-premise or virtual infrastructure directly to Cloudflare. Interconnects allow you to bypass the public Internet for improved network speed, lower packet loss, and reduced latency.
magic.networks.pages.connections.cloud.d: Connect your cloud networks to Cloudflare. First, integrate your cloud providers. Then, create cloud on-ramps from your resources. You can connect a single virtual private cloud (VPC) or a create a hub that includes multiple VPCs.
magic.networks.pages.connections.applian: Install hardware or virtual appliances in your corporate network locations to automatically connect, steer, and shape any IP traffic.  After registering your appliance, create profiles to manage their network configurations.
magic.networks.pages.firewall.page.descr: Monitor risks and apply firewall rules for on-premise, cloud-hosted, and hybrid networks. Delivers scalable firewall protection at every Cloudflare data center.
magic.networks.pages.firewall.page.title: Firewall
magic.networks.pages.settings.page.title: Settings
magic.networks.pages.routes.page.descrip: Manage and review all routes across your Tunnel, Mesh, and WAN connectors.
magic.networks.pages.routes.page.title: Routes
magic.networks.pages.routes.page.wan-con: Set configurations that determine how Cloudflare connects with your resources and infrastructure.
magic.tunnels.traceroute.info: To initiate a traceroute, ensure the target is a public IP.
magic.tunnels.traceroute_error.no_valid_: Invalid traceroute target. Colo must be specified and IP must be public.
insights.health.network.title: Network health
insights.health.network.description: Monitor the health of the sites, WAN tunnels, and interconnects you connected to Cloudflare. Run diagnostics to collect packet-level data of network traffic.
insights.health.network.overview.title: Network overview
insights.health.network.overview.descrip: Connect your traffic sources to configure policies and monitor network health.
insights.health.diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
insights.health.diagnostics.description: Collect packet level data of your network traffic. Sample packet captures collect historical data, while full packet captures monitor active traffic matching your selected filters. To run a full packet capture, you must add a cloud storage bucket.
insights.health.connector.description: Monitor the status of GRE/IPsec tunnels and interconnects that route traffic from Cloudflare to your origin network.
magic.connector.registration.page.title: Add an appliance
magic.connector.registration.page.descri: Enter the serial number(s) associated with your order. Then, go to Networking > Connectors > Appliances to add WAN and LAN configurations.
magic.connector.registration.add_page.de: Enter the serial numbers for the appliance(s) you want to connect. We recommend adding a note with details of your appliance’s location.
magic.connector.registration.add_page.no: Note: You can locate serial numbers via the sticker on your appliance. For the VEP1425, this is the seven-character Dell Service Tag.
magic.connector.registration.add_page.ma: You may register at most %{MAX_DEVICES} appliances at a time.
magic.connector.registration.done_page.d: Review the details of your appliance(s), then make profile configurations.
magic.connector.registration.done_page.b: Unfortunately, the following (%{failedTotal}) appliance(s) could not be registered: <0>%{list}</0>. Please ensure the information provided is correct before attempting to register appliances.
magic.connector.registration.done_page.n: Define how your appliance connects to Cloudflare.
magic.mconn.visibility.noApplianceIdOrDa: We were not able to find an appliance id or fetch data related to it.
magic.mconn.visibility.viewDiscoveryAnal: View discovery analytics associated with this appliance
magic.mconn.visibility.searchAndFilterEv: Search and filter events logged from your appliance.
magic.mconn.visibility.reviewDhcpAssignm: Review current DHCP assignments for your appliance. Lease time remaining reflects how long the address will remain valid.
magic.mconn.visibility.reviewPortData: Review data related to the packets sent through this port. For more detail review the packets troubleshooting checklist.
magic.mconn.visibility.reviewPerformance: Review your appliance’s performance over time.
magic.mconn.visibility.criticalTemperatu: The critical temperature of the appliance is %{criticalTemp} degrees Celsius
magic.mconn.visibility.port_details_no_p: No ports are currently configured on this appliance.
magic.mconn.visibility.port_details_conf: Configured ports list with the status and IP addresses.
magicLib.cloud.name: Name
magic.networks.pages.overview.no_entitle: Explore Cloudflare's network security and connectivity solutions.
Explore more ways to manage your network
Connect your resources to Cloudflare without them being publicly reachable.
Gain real-time visibility into traffic volume from your routers.
Map your dedicated static IPs or BYOIP prefixes to specific hostnames.
Protect your networks, data centers, and private cloud infrastructure from DDoS attacks.
Connect and secure your branch offices, data centers, and cloud resources through Cloudflare's global network.
magic.networks.pages.overview.title: Network overview
magic.networks.pages.overview.transit.su: Secure and improve performance for private, on-premise, cloud-hosted, and hybrid public-facing networks.
magic.networks.pages.overview.transit.ba: This checklist guides you through setting up Magic Transit. Checking off items tracks your progress here, but it does not change your actual configurations.
magic.networks.pages.overview.transit.on: Progress tracking for Magic Transit onboarding is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
Failed to update onboarding task status.
Set up and configure Magic Transit to protect your network infrastructure with Cloudflare.
Verify and prepare all requirements before starting the Magic Transit onboarding process.
You can bring your own IP addresses (BYOIP) to Cloudflare. If you plan to use leased Cloudflare-owned IPs, you can skip this step.
Connect your network infrastructure directly to Cloudflare using tunnels and/or Cloudflare Network Interconnects (CNI).
Routes tell Cloudflare how to steer traffic to your network. Add them via static routes, or use BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) to update routes automatically.
Validate the health of your configuration before you go live.
Advertising your prefixes tells the Internet to route traffic through Cloudflare. Turn on advertisements when you are ready to go live.
Fine-tune your DDoS protection, review traffic analytics, monitor network health, and more.
Bring your own /24 or larger IP block, or use leased Cloudflare-owned IPs.
Add fixed paths to your routing table. Best for stable networks.
Confirm that the connections between your network and Cloudflare are established.
Monitor tunnels that route traffic from Cloudflare to your origin. Magic Transit uses these checks to steer traffic to the best route.
Evaluate connectivity from Cloudflare's data centers to your origin network.
Verify each IRR entry includes the route, origin ASN, and source.
Verify that MSS clamping is set to prevent packet fragmentation in tunnels.
Advertise or withdraw prefixes manually from the Cloudflare dashboard.
Control prefix advertisement programmatically. Best for automation.
Announce or withdraw prefixes directly from your router via BGP.
Advertise prefixes automatically when certain traffic thresholds are met.
Cloudflare advertises when it learns matching routes from your network.
Set AS prepends on a BGP prefix
Make a route less preferred to gradually shift traffic between providers.
Probe IPs deep in your network to check end-to-end reachability.
Monitor your IPsec/GRE tunnels and steer traffic away from failures.
Set how often Cloudflare probes your tunnels: low, mid, or high.
Get notified when tunnel health drops below a threshold you set.
Configure thresholds to detect and block known network-layer attacks.
Stop spoofed and out-of-state floods with stateful TCP inspection.
Protect against sophisticated and randomized DNS-based DDoS attacks.
View traffic patterns and DDoS activity across your network in real time.
Map the hop-by-hop path and latency from Cloudflare to your network.
Capture and analyze packet-level data for troubleshooting.
Routers must support GRE/IPsec tunnels, maximum segment size (MSS) clamping, and asymmetric traffic flow.
Register your IP prefixes in an IRR database. The origin ASN must match your prefix.
Configure MSS clamping on your routers to prevent packet fragmentation in tunnels.
Cloudflare needs your prefix-to-ASN mapping to advertise routes on your behalf. List each IP prefix and the ASN it will originate from.
Please wait for IP prefixes validation

Cloudflare will confirm prefix ownership through your IRR, Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI), and verification token records. This takes about 10 minutes. Prefix advertisement unlocks about 5 hours later. While you wait, you can review the auto-created BGP prefix.
Simpler setup with no encryption. Best for trusted networks or CNI.
Encrypts and authenticates traffic. Requires a pre-shared key to set up.
A physical fiber connection to Cloudflare in a shared data center.
A private virtual connection through a global connectivity partner.
A private connection from your cloud provider directly to Cloudflare.
Automatically exchange routes over your interconnect. Currently in closed beta.
Automatically exchange routes over your tunnels.
magic.networks.pages.overview.transit.re: Set up notifications for tunnel health status changes.
Configure firewall rules to protect your network infrastructure.

magic.routes.unified.header.title: Your routes
magic.routes.unified.header.description: Manage and review all routes across your Tunnel, Mesh, and WAN connectors.
magic.routes.unified.filters.search_plac: Search by prefix, hostname, or description...
magic.routes.unified.type_tooltip.wan_st: WAN route — steers traffic from Cloudflare to your network via GRE, IPsec, or CNI tunnels. Includes static and BGP-learned routes.
magic.routes.unified.type_tooltip.tunnel: Tunnel CIDR route — routes private IP traffic through a Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) to reach resources in your network.
magic.routes.unified.type_tooltip.mesh_c: Mesh CIDR route — routes private IP traffic through a Cloudflare Mesh node for service-to-service or user-to-service connectivity.
magic.routes.unified.type_tooltip.tunnel: Tunnel hostname route — resolves a private hostname through a Cloudflare Tunnel, accessible via the Cloudflare One Client or Gateway.
magic.routes.unified.type_tooltip.mesh_h: Mesh hostname route — resolves a private hostname through a Cloudflare Mesh node, accessible via the Cloudflare One Client or Gateway.
magic.routes.unified.type_tooltip.system: Reserved system subnet — managed by Cloudflare. Used for internal addressing (Cloudflare Mesh IPs, Cloudflare source IPs). Cannot be deleted.
magic.routes.unified.mk2_banner: Your account uses the legacy routing engine where Tunnel routes always take precedence over WAN routes regardless of prefix length. <0>Upgrade to Unified Routing</0> to enable longest-prefix-match evaluation.

magic.routes.unified.delete.warning: This action cannot be undone. The route will be permanently deleted.
magic.routes.unified.delete.confirm_chec: I understand this will delete the route
magic.routes.unified.delete.error: Failed to delete route. Please try again.
magic.routes.unified.empty.title: No routes configured
magic.routes.unified.empty.description: Routes define how traffic reaches your private network. Create a Tunnel or Mesh node to get started.
magic.routes.unified.test_route.title: Test a route
magic.routes.unified.test_route.hint: Type an IP address to see which route would match.
magic.routes.unified.test_route.mode_mk2: Using legacy routing (Tunnel routes take precedence).
magic.routes.unified.test_route.no_match: No route matches this IP address.
magic.routes.unified.test_route.reason_l: Matches because /%{prefixLen} is the most specific prefix.
magic.routes.unified.test_route.reason_e: Traffic is distributed across %{count} routes with the same /%{prefixLen} prefix and priority (ECMP).
magic.routes.unified.test_route.reason_m: Tunnel routes take precedence over WAN routes on the legacy routing engine.
magic.routes.unified.create.title: Create route
magic.routes.unified.create.select_type: What type of route do you want to create?
Route traffic to a private IP range through a Cloudflare Tunnel.
magic.routes.unified.create.tunnel_hostn: Route traffic to a private hostname through a Cloudflare Tunnel.
magic.routes.unified.create.mesh_cidr.de: Route traffic to a private IP range through a Mesh node.
magic.routes.unified.create.mesh_hostnam: Route traffic to a private hostname through a Mesh node.
magic.routes.unified.create.wan_static.d: Create a static route for your Cloudflare WAN or Magic Transit network.
magic.routes.unified.create.weight_toolt: Controls traffic distribution when multiple routes share the same prefix and priority (ECMP). Higher weight values receive proportionally more traffic. Maximum value is 256.
magic.routes.unified.create.region_toolt: Scope this route to specific Cloudflare data center regions. When unset, the route applies globally. Ensure all prefixes have routes covering all regions to avoid traffic drops.
magic.routes.unified.create.network_tool: Enter a network address in CIDR notation (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24). This defines the IP range that will be routed through the selected connector.
magic.routes.unified.create.hostname_too: Enter a private hostname (e.g. wiki.internal.local). Traffic to this hostname will be routed through the selected connector via the Cloudflare One Client.
magic.routes.unified.create.gateway_noti: This route requires traffic to pass through Cloudflare Gateway. Users need the <0>Cloudflare One Client</0> or a similar on-ramp to reach private hostnames. <1>Learn more</1>.
magic.routes.unified.create.no_tunnels_d: You need at least one Cloudflare Tunnel before you can create a Tunnel route. Tunnels are managed under Networking > Tunnels.
magic.routes.unified.create.no_mesh_node: You need at least one Cloudflare Mesh node before you can create a Mesh route. Mesh nodes are managed under Networking > Mesh.
magic.routes.unified.create.docs_link: View documentation for this route type
magic.routes.unified.create.error.networ: Enter a valid CIDR notation (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24 or fd00::/64).
magic.routes.unified.create.error.hostna: Enter a valid hostname (e.g. wiki.internal.local). A single leading wildcard is allowed (e.g. *.internal.local).
magic.routes.unified.create.error.weight: Weight must be a whole number between 1 and 256.
magic.routes.unified.create.error.server: Failed to create route. Please try again.
magic.routes.unified.vnet.title: Virtual networks
magic.routes.unified.vnet.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the virtual network "%{name}"? Routes assigned to this network will become unreachable.
magic.routes.unified.vnet.is_default_des: New routes are automatically assigned to the default virtual network unless specified otherwise.
magic.routes.unified.vnet.error: Failed to save virtual network. Please try again.
magic.routes.unified.vnet.tab_descriptio: New routes are automatically assigned to the default virtual network unless specified otherwise.
magic.routes.unified.vnet.no_results: No virtual networks match your search.
magic.routes.unified.create.vnet_descrip: Assign this route to a virtual network. Routes in different virtual networks can have overlapping CIDRs.
magic.routes.unified.map.capped: Showing %{shown} of %{total} routes. Use filters to narrow the view.
magic.ipsec.custom_ike_id.no_tunnels_ban: To add a custom IKE ID label, edit this tunnel after you create it. Custom labels require at least one existing IPsec tunnel.
